# wanderd

> wanderd is a free visual Entity-Relationship Diagram (ERD) editor for VS Code, Cursor, and macOS. Design database schemas on an interactive canvas, import/export SQL across PostgreSQL, MySQL and SQLite, and let AI assistants build diagrams via the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

## What it is
- Visual ERD / database schema editor.
- Available as a VS Code extension, a Cursor / Open VSX extension, and a standalone macOS desktop app (Apple Silicon).
- Stores diagrams as `.wand` files — human-readable JSON (tables, columns, relationships, indexes, canvas layout) that produces clean diffs for version control.
- Free to use.

## Key features
- Interactive ERD canvas: drag-and-drop tables, pan/zoom, minimap.
- SQL import/export (DDL) with constraints, indexes, defaults.
- Multi-dialect: PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite — automatic column-type conversion.
- Rich table editor: primary keys, nullable, unique, defaults, column/table comments.
- Foreign-key relationships with cardinality (1:1, 1:n, n:m) and cascade rules.
- AI / MCP integration: a built-in Model Context Protocol server lets Claude, Cursor, Gemini, and Codex create and edit diagrams programmatically.

## Install & download
- Website: https://surri.github.io/wanderd/
- VS Code Marketplace: https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=wanderd.wanderd
- Open VSX (Cursor, VSCodium, Kiro): https://open-vsx.org/extension/wanderd/wanderd
- macOS app (Apple Silicon, .dmg): https://github.com/surri/wanderd/releases/latest/download/WandERD-mac-arm64.dmg
- Source & releases: https://github.com/surri/wanderd

## FAQ
- Is it free? Yes.
- Databases supported? PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite.
- Works in Cursor? Yes, via Open VSX.
- Runs on macOS? Yes, signed Apple Silicon desktop app.
- What is a .wand file? Human-readable JSON schema document used by wanderd.
